Housing Supply Elasticity

Per-state OLS, 2010–2024: level–level and log–log

Raw Data: Level–Level and Log–Log

One dot per state-year. The line is a single OLS fit across all observations; per-state slopes (computed above) are the basis for the choropleth and bar charts.

Choropleths

Ranked Bar Charts

Scatter: Elasticity vs. Average Supply

Do states with higher log-log elasticity actually permit more housing? The dashed line marks unit elasticity (β = 1): supply growing proportionally with prices.
